events.description: Detecting 50 failed login attempts from a single IP address (192.168.1.100) indicates an active brute-force or credential stuffing attempt against the cloud environment.
events.action: Temporarily block the source IP address, enforce Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA), and audit access logs to ensure no unauthorized logins succeeded.

events.description: An internal host (10.0.0.55) has downloaded an anomalously large volume of data (15.5 GB), which may indicate data exfiltration or a compromised internal asset.
events.action: Isolate host 10.0.0.55 from the network immediately, identify the asset owner, and review database/file access logs to determine the sensitivity of the downloaded data.

events.description: An internal IP address (172.16.0.200) was detected performing a rapid port scan of 1,000 ports, indicating potential host compromise and lateral movement reconnaissance.
events.action: Isolate the source host 172.16.0.200 immediately, terminate active unauthorized sessions, and conduct a forensic review of the device's system logs.
